GT LEARN #253 🏢 Meeting Rooms II MEDIUM Heap Sort Greedy
intervals= exp: 2

📝 Problem Statement

Diye gaye meeting intervals jahan har interval [start, end] hai — minimum number of conference rooms nikalna hai jo saari meetings ko bina overlap conduct kar sake.

🧠 Solving Approach

Pehle meetings ko start time se sort karo. Phir ek min-heap rakho jisme har currently busy room ka end_time store hoga. Har new meeting pe — agar heap ka top end ≤ current start, toh woh room free ho gaya (pop), warna ek naya room chahiye. Heap mein current end push karo.

🎯 Core Concepts

Sort by start: Earliest meetings first — taaki greedy decisions sahi ho.
Min-heap of end times: Heap top = earliest-ending currently busy room. Yahi check karna hai — kya yeh free ho gaya?
Final answer: Heap ki len() = simultaneously active rooms ka peak.
⏱️ Time: O(N log N) 💾 Space: O(N)

🧪 Test Cases (click to load)

classic[0,30] overlaps with both→ 2
intervals=[[0,30],[5,10],[15,20]]
no-overlapDisjoint — one room enough→ 1
intervals=[[7,10],[2,4]]
tied-startTwo start at same time→ 2
intervals=[[1,5],[8,9],[8,9]]
touch-edgeend==next start (no overlap)→ 2
intervals=[[9,10],[4,9],[4,17]]
stressHeavy overlap — 4 rooms→ 4
intervals=[[1,10],[2,7],[3,19],[8,12],[10,20],[11,30]]
—
📜 Python Code
📊 Visualization
0/0 —
←→R 100%
